items.title: Defer tool loading so agents search for tools instead of hauling them into every prompt
items.why: Large MCP and tool catalogs crush context windows and hurt tool selection; on-demand discovery preserves prompt cacheability and improves accuracy.
how_to_steps: Keep only a small always-loaded core toolset and add a Tool Search tool to the initial prompt so the agent starts with discovery rather than a giant schema dump.
Mark the long tail of tools or even whole MCP servers with defer_loading so they are discovered on demand instead of occupying context from turn one.
Keep the shared system prompt and core tools byte-stable across calls so prompt caching still hits while deferred tools are pulled in only after search.
Measure token use and tool-selection accuracy before and after the change, then promote only the tools that are truly used on nearly every task back into the always-loaded set.
items.evidence: Anthropic says Tool Search cut context usage from roughly 77K tokens to about 8.7K, preserved 95% of the context window, reduced token usage 85%, and improved MCP-eval accuracy from 49% to 74% on Opus 4 and from 79.5% to 88.1% on Opus 4.5.
items.try_today: Take one 20-plus-tool setup, defer-load the rarely used tools, and compare prompt size plus tool-choice accuracy on the next five real tasks.
